title: Catch wording that drifts between work items
summary: Give karta a deterministic way to enforce that a string several work items must render identically actually stays identical. A binder can declare canonical shared terms, and a new check halts a delivery whose assembled result lets one drift — closing the gap the binder end-of-life dogfood found.
motivation: Per-item gates each see one item's diff, so nothing catches two items wording the same thing differently; this adds the missing whole-binder check.

included: A new optional `shared_terms` field on the binder (canonical substring + the items that must render it)
Schema validation of that field in validate_binder.py, and its documentation in binder-reference.md
A new deterministic check_shared_terms.py that enforces declared terms on the assembled tree
karta-plan surfacing candidate shared terms at plan time
karta-deliver and karta-build invoking the check and halting on drift
A pointer from the house stack pack's term-identity rule to the new mechanism
excluded: Any heuristic / fuzzy near-duplicate scanner (explicitly rejected in the design — v1 is declared-terms only)
A code-level canonical-source rule (single-definition + import) — breaks the stack-agnostic constraint
Changing the per-item safety or acceptance gates (they structurally cannot see cross-item drift)

Declaring this binder's own shared_terms — the current validator predates the field, so the deliver and build items keep the check-invocation wording identical via their dependency edge (build quotes deliver) rather than a shared_terms entry

design_facts.stack: karta plugin repo — Python 3.11+ stdlib scripts run via `uv run --script`, each with an embedded `--self-test` ([PASS]/[FAIL] + `N/N checks passed`); Markdown SKILL.md skills with 3-way byte-identical mirrors (skills/ -> .agents/skills/ -> plugins/karta/skills/) regenerated by sync_codex_skills.py; JSON binder files. Ships as a Claude Code plugin and a Codex CLI plugin.

work_items.title: Define and validate the shared-terms field
work_items.summary: Add the optional shared_terms field to the binder, validate its shape at plan time, and document it in the binder reference.
work_items.contract: validate_binder.py accepts an optional top-level `shared_terms` array. Each entry has `id` (kebab, unique across entries), `canonical` (non-empty string), and `items` (>= 2 work-item ids that all resolve to real items in this binder). A dangling item id is an error (mirroring the depends_on check); an entry whose listed item has empty `touches` draws a warning (the deliver-time check would have nothing to scan). An absent or empty `shared_terms` is valid and a no-op. binder-reference.md documents the field, its three keys, and that `canonical` is a byte-identical substring (not a whole line), so interpolated prefixes stay outside it.

oracle.command: uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/validate_binder.py --self-test && uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: a well-formed shared_terms entry validates; a dangling item id errors; a duplicate id errors; an empty canonical errors; a single-item entry errors
an item with empty touches listed in a shared_terms entry warns
the karta-plan 3-way mirror is byte-identical and validate_plugin passes

work_items.title: Build the shared-terms enforcement check
work_items.summary: Add a deterministic script that asserts each declared canonical term appears byte-identical in every listed item's touched files.
work_items.contract: New house-pattern script skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py (pure stdlib, argparse, embedded --self-test with [PASS]/[FAIL] and an `N/N checks passed` tally). Inputs: `--binder <path>` and a repo root (default cwd). Per shared_terms entry: gather each listed item's `touches` files that exist under the root; if any listed item has no existing touched files the entry is [PENDING] (skipped — that item is not delivered yet); otherwise each listed item must have `canonical` as a byte-identical substring in at least one of its existing touched files, and an item whose files exist but lack it is a violation. Any violation -> [FAIL] naming the entry id, offending item(s), and the canonical string, exit non-zero. All satisfied-or-pending -> exit 0. Matching is exact substring over file bytes; no similarity threshold, no language assumptions. An absent/empty shared_terms is a clean no-op pass.

oracle.command: uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py --self-test && uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: a term all items render identically passes; one item drifting fails and names the item + canonical
an entry whose item has no touched files on the tree is [PENDING] (skipped, not failed)
a binder with no shared_terms is a clean no-op pass
the new script is mirrored 3-way and validate_plugin passes

work_items.title: Surface shared-term candidates at plan time
work_items.summary: Teach karta-plan to propose shared_terms candidates from item contracts so the human can confirm them into the binder.
work_items.contract: karta-plan's SKILL.md gains a `plan:terms` step in synthesis: the subagent proposes shared_terms entries from (a) a contract that says an item must quote or match another item's string verbatim, and (b) two or more items whose contracts describe emitting the same user-facing string. Candidates surface in the plan review card for the human to confirm; surfacing is advisory, not a gate. The step states plainly that this is the only point with cross-item visibility and that it is best-effort, not a guarantee.

oracle.command: uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: the karta-plan 3-way mirror is byte-identical (sync --check reports no drift)
SKILL.md describes the plan:terms surfacing step and its two candidate signals

work_items.title: Run the check when delivering a wave
work_items.summary: Wire the shared-terms check into karta-deliver's post-wave step so a delivery halts when a declared term drifts.
work_items.contract: karta-deliver's SKILL.md runs `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py` on the integration tip as part of the post-wave check (deliver:waveloop step 4), after the build/type-check. A [FAIL] halts the wave on the same footing as a failed post-wave build (revert or fix); [PENDING] entries (items in later waves) are skipped and re-evaluated when those waves land. The invocation command is quoted exactly as the binder's `check-invocation` shared term.

oracle.command: uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: the post-wave step invokes check_shared_terms.py and halts the wave on [FAIL]

work_items.title: Run the check in the single-item build path
work_items.summary: Wire the same check into karta-build's single-item hatch and point the house pack's term rule at the new mechanism.
work_items.contract: karta-build's SKILL.md single-item hatch runs `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py` before its merge completes, quoting the invocation exactly as karta-deliver does (the binder's `check-invocation` shared term). The house stack pack `.karta/sme/karta-house-skill-authoring.md` adds a pointer from its 'state every cross-file term identically' rule to shared_terms as the enforcing mechanism; the pack stays valid (guard_pack_write / validate_plugin).

oracle.command: uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: validate_plugin --self-test passes (including the edited overlay pack)
the single-item hatch invokes check_shared_terms.py with the same wording as karta-deliver
the house pack points its term-identity rule at shared_terms
